title: AMD to Invest Up to $5 Billion in Anthropic, Deploy Chips
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. has agreed to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic PBC as part of a potential multi-gigawatt deployment of chips for the artificial intelligence startup, deepening ties between the two firms and aiding the Claude maker’s push to meet surging AI demand.

title: BAE Launches Autonomous Combat Jet Program for UK, Export
summary: BAE Systems Plc unveiled an uncrewed combat jet that’s been in development since 2022, another entrant in an increasingly crowded field as the UK and Europe seek to build sovereign defense capabilities without US help.

title: Miami Is Losing Its Cost Advantage Over NYC
summary: Plus: Record heat weighs on London’s office market, and a New York sidewalk eyesore is due for a glow up

title: Aston Martin Raises £550 Million Debt From HPS to Boost Cash
summary: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc has struck a deal for £550 million ($736 million) of fresh funds with BlackRock Inc.-owned HPS Investment Partners to bolster its liquidity.

title: Stocks Churn Before Big-Tech Earnings as Oil Jumps: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street investors bracing for the start of the megacap earnings season sent stocks wavering while worries about an escalation of the Iran war boosted oil prices.

title: Kuwait Is Set to Sell $6 Billion Bond Even as Iran Strikes
summary: Kuwait is set to raise $6 billion from a three-part dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone attacks from Iran.

title: Prologis Wins Segro Backing for £14 Billion Takeover
summary: Prologis Inc. is set to acquire the UK’s largest landlord Segro Plc after securing the recommendation of its board for an improved £14 billion ($18.7 billion) cash and share offer for the company.

title: Japan May Move to Managing Yields, Not Yen, Deutsche Bank Says
summary: Japan may have to shift its focus from supporting the yen toward controlling government bond yields if it is to achieve its ambitious economic growth plans, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

title: BofA Equities Traders Say Buy Momentum Stocks, Eyeing 5% Gain
summary: Bank of America Corp.’s trading desk is urging clients to buy US momentum stocks, betting that the group has reached an attractive price level after a bout of profit taking.

title: GE Vernova Falls as Outlook Clashes With ‘Too High’ AI Hopes
summary: GE Vernova Inc.’s shares slid after a higher revenue outlook failed to impress investors, a sign that it’s getting harder for companies that sell equipment to data centers to meet the market’s sky-high hopes.

title: Wall Street Won’t Give Up on Saylor’s Strategy, Sees 170% Upside
summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. is confronting a collapse of confidence almost everywhere — except among Wall Street analysts.

title: Vale Elects Ollie Chairman After Weeks of Turmoil
summary: Vale SA shareholders elected Manuel Lino Silva de Sousa Oliveira as chairman on Wednesday, concluding weeks of boardroom turmoil sparked by an effort from one of the Brazilian mining giant’s largest investors to reshape the company’s leadership.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
summary: Oil briefly spiked above $95, extending this month’s price jump to around 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of peace talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

title: The AI Export Boom That Wasn’t
summary: Thailand’s tech exports are booming but so is its trade deficit.

title: Stumbling Stock Rally Looks to Earnings for Second Half Boost
summary: Investors who spent weeks watching US stocks go nowhere are pinning their hopes for a breakout on a key pillar of the four-year bull run: corporate earnings.

title: Houthi Threats Give Oil Markets a ‘Two Chokepoint Problem’
summary: Ships continue to turn away from the southern end of the Red Sea, after Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels announced an embargo on Saudi exports through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a narrow channel that has become a lifeline for oil exports since the beginning of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

title: S&P 500 Fluctuates as Traders Brace for Alphabet, Tesla Results
summary: US stocks swung between small gains and losses after chipmakers came off session lows and oil prices jumped on the escalation of the US-Iran war. Traders are also bracing for key earnings from Alphabet Inc., Tesla Inc. and International Business Machines Corp. due later in the afternoon.

title: Heat and War Deal Europe’s Grain Harvest Biggest Blow in Decades
summary: Europe is set to suffer one of its sharpest ever declines in grain production as extreme weather and geopolitical tensions converge to pile pressure on farmers.

title: Gold Advances as Dip-Buying Outweighs War, Inflation Worries
summary: Gold extended gains, supported by dip-buyers who scooped up the metal after prices have drifted lower in recent weeks on concerns that US inflationary pressure will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

title: EasyJet Shares Slump After Report on EU Ownership Rules Review
summary: EasyJet Plc shares fell the most in four years on Wednesday after Reuters reported that the European Union is preparing to review airline ownership rules that it said might affect takeover bids for the low-cost carrier by two US investment firms.

title: This ETF Tax Loophole Is All the Rage Among Rich Americans
summary: A bespoke fund is becoming a must-have tool for wealthy investors looking to defer — and sometimes even avoid — capital gains tax.

title: Zambia’s World-Beating 36% Local Bond Rally to Extend After Polls, Citi Says
summary: The world-beating rally in Zambia’s bonds is likely to extend if President Hakainde Hichilema decisively wins next month’s elections, according to Citigroup Inc.

title: Galaxy Digital Seeks $3.5 Billion of Debt for Texas Data Center
summary: Galaxy Digital Inc. is planning to raise about $3.5 billion from a debut junk-bond sale to fund a data center tied to CoreWeave Inc., extending AI financing’s push into the riskier corners of the US credit market.
